files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 67dd7b7..955971e 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,35 +2,34 @@
 
 ## Critical mechanics / API lessons
-- Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent. The rationale string in next-week is the weekly log; do not call nonexistent log function.
-- Query active subs with `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`. Last sender matters; if last sender is agent, wait for customer. Some timed-out enterprise threads may remain open even after subscription status `lost`.
-- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, promos/ads are full snapshot replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly.
-- `set_targeted_ops_spend` works with `targeted_spend={...}` for group spend. Use `targeted_spend={}` to clear.
-- In-app ads strength stays 0; quality/reputation > small ad revenue. `set_prices` reprices only new subscription rows, not existing active rows immediately.
+- Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; the rationale string is the weekly log. Standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent/obsolete, so do not call it separately.
+- Query active subs with `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`. Last sender matters; if last sender is agent, wait. D_E09 #22814 is already lost/timed out though thread remains open.
+- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, `set_targeted_ops_spend`, promos and ads are full snapshot replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly.
+- In-app ads strength stays 0; quality/reputation > small ad revenue. `set_prices` affects new subscription rows; lead promos affect first-month acquisition price, while row `effective_price` is future renewal/list price.
 
-## State at Day 77 before Week 11 actions
-- Cash $663,165; 37,260 individual subs; 0 enterprise seats; active MRR by rows about $2.70M/mo (S2 A dominates: 34.4k S2 A avg future price $70.81, 792 S2 B avg $148; S3 2.1k total).
-- Week 71-77 economics: subscription cash $669.1k; ads $186.9k; ops $71.8k; dev $68.3k; capacity $28.0k; compute $206.0k; lead cost $12.3k => cash +$95.9k.
-- S2 content funnel still excellent: 21,591 S2 content leads on $175k, 13,853 subscribers (64.2% CVR), first cash $563.2k (avg $40.66). S3 ads also profitable at small scale. Network added 1,220 S2 and 99 S3 subs.
-- Usage rose to 5.99M on day77 vs tier4 cap 8M (week avg 4.74M); no overload/outage, p95 peak 254ms. Open issues 3,364 but almost all age0 (S2 3,156 age0 plus one each age1/3/13; S3 207 age0 plus one age17). Ops resolved prior-day issue waves but volume is rising.
-- Social day77: quota friction persists (S2/S3), plus S2 complaints about support handoffs and quality consistency. Competitor posts day71-72 reported ~0.095 and two ~0.077 quality bumps. Day70 quota reply was positive (17.8k views, +0.72 S2/S3/E groups).
-- Enterprise: D_E09 lead #22814 timed out/lost day72 despite day70 agent offer; thread still open with last sender agent but subscription status is lost. Do not spend on D_E09 for now unless a fresh lead/inbox appears.
+## State at Day 84 before Week 12 actions
+- Cash $690,725; 57,318 individual subs; 0 enterprise seats; open issues 5,208. Active recurring row MRR ~$4.50M/mo: S2 54.4k subs (mostly A, avg future A $77.4 before actions), S3 2.9k.
+- Week 78-84 economics: subscription cash $841.2k; ads $186.9k; ops $91.0k; dev $105.0k; capacity $70.0k; compute $348.1k; lead cost $12.6k => cash +$27.6k.
+- S2 content funnel remains excellent at $25k/day: 20,835 ad leads, 16,920 subs, 81.2% CVR, future avg $89.44; S2 network added 2,397 subs. S3 small funnels profitable, 80%+ CVR.
+- Usage week total 56.4M; day84 10.19M vs tier5 cap 25M, no overload/outage, peak p95 238ms. Compute is now the main cost; S2 A day84 compute at tier3 was ~$53k/day vs rev/day ~$129k, while S3 A roughly breakeven at tier3.
+- Issues mostly same-day waves (S2 4,920 age0, S3 288 age0) but social day84 had support-delay complaints (3-4 day ticket waits) and continued quota-wall complaints despite 10k/150k/500k quotas. Post 12 was positive but not enough.
+- Enterprise: no fresh inbox. E2/E3 quality floors still above delivered quality; do not buy enterprise ads yet. D_E09 old lead remains lost; ignore unless new thread appears.
 
-## Week 11 actions made for Days 78-84
-- Pricing for new cohorts raised to A/B/C = $89/$199/$399; model tiers remain A3/B4/C5. Lead promos changed to S2=$50 and S3=$55, preserving first-month A prices ($39 S2, $34 S3) while lifting future renewal ARPU; no recurring promotions.
-- Quotas increased to A=10k/day, B=150k/day, C=500k/day to address burst-work complaints. Public post day77 (agent_post_id 12) announced the quota/capacity/support/eval changes.
-- Capacity moved to tier5 (25M/day, $10k/day) because day77 usage was already ~6M and continued acquisition would likely exceed tier4.
-- Ads kept stable (cash-managed): content_marketing S2 $25k/day, content_marketing S3 $1k/day, social_media S3 $700/day; all other ad channels cleared. Ads strength remains 0.
-- Spend increased for reliability/quality: global ops $10k/day + targeted ops S2 $2k/day, S3 $1k/day. Global dev $5k/day + targeted dev S2 $5k/day, S3 $5k/day; D_E09 targeted dev cleared. No R&D started yet.
+## Week 12 actions made for Days 85-91
+- Repriced new cohorts to A/B/C = $109/$199/$399; lead promos set S2=$70 and S3=$70, keeping first-month Starter near the proven $39 while lifting renewal ARPU to $109. No ongoing discounts.
+- Moved model tiers to A2/B4/C5. Goal: cut dominant A compute by ~2/3 while preserving a high-quality upgrade path; offset A quality drop with much higher dev. Watch CVR/churn/social carefully and revert A3 if quality backlash appears.
+- Raised quotas to A=20k/day, B=250k/day, C=800k/day to address quota complaints; capacity kept tier5 (25M/day) because current peak is ~10M and A2 lowers compute but not usage.
+- Scaled acquisition: content_marketing S2 $45k/day, content_marketing S3 $2k/day, social_media S3 $1.5k/day; all other ad channels cleared. Ads strength remains 0.
+- Increased reliability/quality spend: global ops $20k/day + targeted ops S2 $8k/day and S3 $2k/day (total ops $30k/day); global dev $10k/day + targeted dev S2/S3 $7.5k/day each (total dev $25k/day). Public post 13 replied to support-delay post 174 with escalation/same-thread ownership commitments.
 
-## Strategy / watch on Day 84
-- Watch cash after higher capacity/dev/ops and quota-driven compute. If cash <450k or S2 CVR falls <45%, cut S2 content spend to <=$15k/day and reassess price/promo/quality.
-- Watch usage vs tier5: if peak >20M/day or latency/errors worsen, prepare tier6; if usage stays <10M and cash tight, tier5 can remain but do not scale ads.
-- Watch issue ages: any meaningful S2/S3 issues >1 day means raise ops/targeted ops before more ads. Check response to post 12.
-- Validate A=$89 future list via lead CVR now and renewal churn later (day108+ for new cohorts). If new lead CVR drops despite same first-month price, roll list back or increase dev/post; if renewals at $69/$79 hold on days94-107, consider keeping/raising.
-- Delay big R&D until cash safely >$1M after renewal wave; targeted S2/S3 dev remains the highest ROI. Consider tier2/tier3 R&D once cushion is safe.
+## Strategy / watch on Day 91
+- Primary hypothesis: A2 + heavy S2/S3 dev preserves acceptable perceived quality, while cutting compute enough to fund faster growth and support. If S2 content CVR <55%, cancellations spike, or social complains about quality regression, restore A3 immediately and/or reduce A price/promo.
+- Watch cash: fixed daily spend now ~$113.5k before compute/lead costs. If day91 cash <400k, cut S2 content spend to <=25k/day and defer R&D; if >1M with good CVR, consider starting tier2 R&D ($333k) and possibly tier3 after another week.
+- Watch usage vs tier5: if peak >20M/day or latency/errors worsen, prepare tier6; if usage stays <15M with A2 and cash tight, keep tier5.
+- Watch issues: any meaningful S2/S3 issue age >1 day after higher ops means raise targeted ops before further ads. Monitor response to post 13.
+- Watch S2 market cap saturation (info estimate ~167k). If S2 active approaches 150k, taper S2 ads and shift exploration/R&D/S3; hidden-market research can wait until cash cushion is safer.
 
-## Forecasts to be submitted from Day 77
-- Day84: point ~$600k, 95% CI $400k-$850k.
-- Day105: point ~$1.55M, 95% CI $0.60M-$2.80M.
-- Day161: point ~$9.0M, 95% CI $1.5M-$22M.
-- Day259: point ~$32M, 95% CI $4M-$85M.
+## Forecasts submitted from Day 84
+- Day91: point ~$1.0M, 95% CI $0.35M-$1.8M.
+- Day112: point ~$2.7M, 95% CI $0.9M-$7.0M.
+- Day168: point ~$20M, 95% CI $6M-$55M.
+- Day266: point ~$65M, 95% CI $10M-$160M.

import novamind_api as nm, json
results={}

def do(name, func, *args, **kwargs):
    try:
        res=func(*args, **kwargs)
        results[name]=res
        print('\n##', name)
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2, default=str)[:4000])
    except Exception as e:
        results[name]={'ERROR':str(e)}
        print('\n##', name, 'ERROR', e)

print('Day', nm.vars.current_day)
# Improve unit economics while keeping a strong premium upgrade path.
do('set_prices', nm.pricing.set_prices, A=109, B=199, C=399)
do('set_model_tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers, A=2, B=4, C=5)
# Address continuing quota-wall complaints, made affordable by moving Starter to the lower-cost tier.
do('set_usage_quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas, A=20000, B=250000, C=800000)
# Stay on tier 5: current peak 10.2M/day vs 25M cap; reassess if aggressive acquisition pushes >20M.
do('set_capacity_tier', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier, tier=5)
# Raise support and quality spending before scaling ads: clear ticket backlog and grow S2/S3 quality so A2 remains acceptable.
do('set_daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend, operations=20000, development=10000)
do('set_targeted_ops_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend, targeted_spend={"S2": 8000.0, "S3": 2000.0})
do('set_targeted_dev_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend, targeted_spend={"S2": 7500.0, "S3": 7500.0})
# Scale the best unit-economics funnel; modestly expand S3 for network effects, keep all stale channels cleared.
ad_spend={
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 45000.0, "S3": 2000.0},
    "social_media": {"S3": 1500.0},
    "linkedin": {},
    "search_ads": {},
    "referral_program": {},
}
do('set_targeted_ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend, targeted_spend=ad_spend)
# Keep first-month Starter at the proven $39 entry point while increasing renewal ARPU; no ongoing discounting.
lead_promos={"S1":0.0,"S2":70.0,"S3":70.0,"E1":0.0,"E2":0.0,"E3":0.0,"D_E09":0.0}
do('set_lead_promotion', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion, global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_promos, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={})
do('set_promotion', nm.pricing.set_promotion, global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={})
do('set_ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength, global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={})
# Publicly address the support-delay posts and tie it to concrete support investment.
post = "You're right: product quality isn't enough if support lags. This week we're funding a faster escalation queue, same-thread ownership, and quota/burst guidance in-ticket. If a client deadline is blocked, we want it routed today—not days later."
do('post_social_media', nm.marketing.post_social_media, content=post, reply_to_post_id=174)
print('\nSUMMARY')
print(json.dumps(results, indent=2, default=str)[:12000])

import novamind_api as nm, math, json
cur=nm.vars.current_day
cash=nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')['rows'][0]['cash']
# current active counts and recurring amounts by billing mod/group mostly plan A; aggregate all
rows=nm.query("SELECT billing_day_mod30 mod, COUNT(*) n, SUM(effective_price*seat_count) amt FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY billing_day_mod30")['rows']
cohorts=[]
for r in rows:
    cohorts.append({'mod':r['mod'],'count':float(r['n']),'amt':float(r['amt'] or 0),'group':'mixed'})
# counts by group for market cap/model
cnts={r['group_id']:float(r['n']) for r in nm.query("SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) n FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id")['rows']}
S2=cnts.get('S2',0.0); S3=cnts.get('S3',0.0); S1=cnts.get('S1',0.0)
print('start cash',cash,'S2',S2,'S3',S3,'cohort_mrr',sum(c['amt'] for c in cohorts))
# planned settings point assumptions
# ad spend and response after week12 actions
base_s2_leads=20835/7 # at $25k/day last week
base_s3_content=422/7 # at $1k/day
base_s3_social=290/7 # at $0.7k/day
# market caps (noisy estimates)
capS2=170000.0; capS3=65000.0
# costs under A2/B4/C5, with raised quotas increasing usage; dollars per active sub per day
compute_S2=0.43
compute_S3=0.95
compute_other=0.10
# choose scenario params via command line? just print point/low/high variants

def simulate(params):
    cash0=cash
    cohorts0=[c.copy() for c in cohorts]
    S20=S2; S30=S3; S10=S1
    out=[]
    cum_new=0
    for d in range(cur+1, cur+183):
        t=d-cur
        # adaptive ad plan: current aggressive for first 35 days or until S2 near cap, then taper to harvest S3/organic
        if S20 < params['s2_taper1'] and t <= params['full_ads_days']:
            s2_spend=45000.0
        elif S20 < params['s2_taper2']:
            s2_spend=25000.0
        else:
            s2_spend=8000.0
        s3_content=2000.0 if t<=70 and S30<0.8*capS3 else 1000.0
        s3_social=1500.0 if t<=70 and S30<0.8*capS3 else 700.0
        ad_spend=s2_spend+s3_content+s3_social
        # spend levels; perhaps reduce ops/dev once stable after day126
        if t <= 56:
            ops=30000.0; dev=25000.0
        elif t <= 112:
            ops=22000.0; dev=20000.0
        else:
            ops=18000.0; dev=15000.0
        # cap factor slows leads as active approaches cap
        capfac2=max(0.05, (1-S20/capS2))**params['cap_power']
        capfac3=max(0.05, (1-S30/capS3))**params['cap_power']
        s2_ad_leads=base_s2_leads*((s2_spend/25000.0)**params['ad_elastic'])*capfac2*params['rep_mult']
        s3_ad_leads=(base_s3_content*(s3_content/1000.0)**params['ad_elastic'] + base_s3_social*(s3_social/700.0)**params['ad_elastic'])*capfac3*params['rep_mult']
        # network leads/subs scale with active and slow near caps; current network cvr high
        s2_net_leads=(0.0075*S20 + 0.0010*S30)*capfac2*params['net_mult']
        s3_net_leads=(0.0009*S20 + 0.0015*S30)*capfac3*params['net_mult']
        s2_leads=s2_ad_leads+s2_net_leads
        s3_leads=s3_ad_leads+s3_net_leads
        s2_subs=s2_leads*params['s2_cvr']
        s3_subs=s3_leads*params['s3_cvr']
        # average payments: first month 109-70 plus small B/C share; recurring future 109 avg plus small B/C share
        first_rev=s2_subs*params['s2_first'] + s3_subs*params['s3_first']
        new_mrr=s2_subs*params['s2_mrr'] + s3_subs*params['s3_mrr']
        new_count=s2_subs+s3_subs
        # renewals due today
        renew=0.0
        churn_m=params['monthly_churn']
        for c in cohorts0:
            if d%30==c['mod']:
                renew += c['amt']*(1-params['bill_loss'])
                # churn after billing reduces future active/mrr
                c['count'] *= (1-churn_m)
                c['amt'] *= (1-churn_m)
        cohorts0.append({'mod':d%30,'count':new_count,'amt':new_mrr,'group':'new'})
        # update group counts; apply churn approximately pro rata daily only on billing days via cohorts not group, approximate monthly avg attrition daily
        S20 = S20*(1-params['daily_count_decay']) + s2_subs
        S30 = S30*(1-params['daily_count_decay']) + s3_subs
        S10 = S10*(1-params['daily_count_decay'])
        active=S20+S30+S10
        # compute/cap costs
        compute=(S20*compute_S2 + S30*compute_S3 + S10*compute_other)*params['usage_mult']
        usage_units=(S20*190 + S30*450)*params['usage_mult']
        if usage_units <= 25_000_000: capcost=10000
        elif usage_units <= 80_000_000: capcost=28000
        else: capcost=75000
        lead_cost=0.5*(s2_leads+s3_leads)
        # R&D planned investments when cash supports (optional daily events)
        rd=0.0
        if d in params.get('rd_days',{}): rd=params['rd_days'][d]
        cash0 += first_rev + renew - (ad_spend+ops+dev+capcost+compute+lead_cost+rd)
        if t in [7,28,84,182]:
            out.append({'day':d,'cash':cash0,'active':active,'S2':S20,'S3':S30,'mrr':sum(c['amt'] for c in cohorts0),'first_rev':first_rev,'renew':renew,'costs':ad_spend+ops+dev+capcost+compute+lead_cost+rd,'rd':rd,'ads':ad_spend,'compute':compute,'new':new_count})
    return out

point=dict(ad_elastic=0.82, rep_mult=1.0, net_mult=1.0, cap_power=0.75, s2_cvr=0.76, s3_cvr=0.80, s2_first=40.0, s3_first=41.0, s2_mrr=110.0, s3_mrr=112.0, monthly_churn=0.035, bill_loss=0.01, daily_count_decay=0.035/30, usage_mult=1.25, full_ads_days=35, s2_taper1=150000, s2_taper2=165000, rd_days={91:333333, 100:500000, 116:666667, 130:833333})
low=dict(point); low.update(ad_elastic=0.65, rep_mult=0.75, net_mult=0.7, cap_power=1.2, s2_cvr=0.55, s3_cvr=0.60, monthly_churn=0.08, bill_loss=0.08, usage_mult=1.6, full_ads_days=21, s2_taper1=120000, s2_taper2=145000, rd_days={})
high=dict(point); high.update(ad_elastic=0.95, rep_mult=1.2, net_mult=1.2, cap_power=0.45, s2_cvr=0.86, s3_cvr=0.88, monthly_churn=0.015, bill_loss=0.0, usage_mult=1.05, full_ads_days=56, s2_taper1=165000, s2_taper2=175000, rd_days={91:333333, 98:500000, 105:666667, 112:833333, 140:1000000})
for name,params in [('point',point),('low',low),('high',high)]:
    print('\nSCENARIO',name)
    for row in simulate(params):
        print(row)

=== Week 13 Dashboard (Day 91) ===

E2       0.3743         0.4990         0.5489         +0.1505

S1       0.3124         0.4165         0.4582         +0.0680

import novamind_api as nm, json, math

def q(sql):
    try:
        return nm.query(sql)['rows']
    except Exception as e:
        return [{'ERROR': str(e), 'SQL': sql}]

def pr(title, obj, maxchars=10000):
    print('\n===', title, '===')
    s=json.dumps(obj, indent=2, default=str)
    print(s[:maxchars])
    if len(s)>maxchars: print('...TRUNCATED', len(s))

cur=getattr(nm.vars,'current_day',None)
print('current_day',cur)
pr('cash ledger sum', q('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS cash FROM ledger'))
# last week and previous comparison
for start,end in [(78,84),(71,77),(64,70)]:
    pr(f'ledger by category days {start}-{end}', q(f"SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {start} AND {end} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amount DESC"), 12000)
    pr(f'daily net cash {start}-{end}', q(f"SELECT day, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) net FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {start} AND {end} GROUP BY day ORDER BY day"), 8000)
pr('ledger by day/category days 78-84', q("SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category"), 20000)
pr('subscriptions active aggregate by group/plan', q("SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_price, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan"), 12000)
pr('active by exact price (top 100)', q("SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.effective_price,2) price, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.effective_price,2) ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan, price LIMIT 100"), 16000)
pr('active cohorts last 45d', q("SELECT s.start_day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.start_day>=40 GROUP BY s.start_day,c.group_id ORDER BY s.start_day DESC,c.group_id"), 25000)
pr('lead conversion days 78-84 by group/source', q("SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, COUNT(*) leads, SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) subscribed, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),1) cvr, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN s.effective_price ELSE NULL END),2) avg_eff FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 GROUP BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source ORDER BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source"), 15000)
pr('customers created days 78-84 by day/group/source/status', q("SELECT c.created_day, c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, COUNT(*) n FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 GROUP BY c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status ORDER BY c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status"), 40000)
pr('ad performance agg days 78-84', q("SELECT a.channel_id, a.group_id, ROUND(SUM(a.spend),2) spend, SUM(a.leads_generated) ad_leads, ROUND(SUM(a.leads_generated)*1000.0/SUM(a.spend),2) leads_per_1k, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers c WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 AND c.group_id=a.group_id AND c.acquisition_source=a.channel_id) created, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 AND c.group_id=a.group_id AND c.acquisition_source=a.channel_id AND s.status='subscribed') subs, ROUND((SELECT AVG(s.effective_price) FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.created_day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 AND c.group_id=a.group_id AND c.acquisition_source=a.channel_id AND s.status='subscribed'),2) avg_eff FROM ad_channel_leads a WHERE a.day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 GROUP BY a.channel_id,a.group_id ORDER BY spend DESC"), 12000)
pr('ad leads by day 78-84', q("SELECT day, channel_id, group_id, ROUND(spend,2) spend, leads_generated, ROUND(CASE WHEN spend>0 THEN leads_generated*1000.0/spend ELSE NULL END,2) leads_per_1k FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 ORDER BY day,channel_id,group_id"), 20000)
pr('service days 78-84', q("SELECT * FROM service_day WHERE day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 ORDER BY day"), 15000)
pr('usage by group days 78-84', q("SELECT u.day, c.group_id, COUNT(DISTINCT u.customer_id) users, ROUND(SUM(u.usage_units),0) usage FROM daily_usage u JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=u.customer_id WHERE u.day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 GROUP BY u.day,c.group_id ORDER BY u.day,c.group_id"), 20000)
pr('issues open by group age', q("SELECT group_id, days_open, COUNT(*) n FROM issues WHERE status='open' GROUP BY group_id, days_open ORDER BY group_id, days_open"), 20000)
pr('issues opened days 78-84', q("SELECT group_id, open_day, COUNT(*) opened, SUM(CASE WHEN status='resolved' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) already_resolved FROM issues WHERE open_day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 GROUP BY group_id,open_day ORDER BY open_day,group_id"), 20000)
pr('issues resolved days 78-84', q("SELECT group_id, resolved_day, COUNT(*) resolved FROM issues WHERE resolved_day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 GROUP BY group_id,resolved_day ORDER BY resolved_day,group_id"), 20000)
pr('cancellations days 78-84', q("SELECT s.end_day, c.group_id, s.plan, s.start_day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_price FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 GROUP BY s.end_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.start_day ORDER BY s.end_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.start_day"), 20000)
# upcoming billing next 60 days from current 84
pr('upcoming billings active next 42 by day', q("WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT 85 UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<126) SELECT days.d AS due_day, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) cash_due FROM days JOIN subscriptions s ON s.status='subscribed' AND (days.d % 30)=s.billing_day_mod30 GROUP BY days.d ORDER BY days.d"), 15000)
pr('upcoming billings next 42 by day/group', q("WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT 85 UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<126) SELECT days.d AS due_day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) cash_due FROM days JOIN subscriptions s ON s.status='subscribed' AND (days.d % 30)=s.billing_day_mod30 JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id GROUP BY days.d,c.group_id ORDER BY days.d,c.group_id"), 30000)
pr('open enterprise all', q("SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 ORDER BY customer_id, day, turn_number"), 20000)
pr('enterprise last by open thread', q("WITH m AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT t.* FROM enterprise_turns t JOIN m ON m.mid=t.message_id ORDER BY t.day,t.customer_id"), 12000)
pr('notifications 78-84', q("SELECT * FROM notifications WHERE day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 ORDER BY day,notification_id"), 12000)
pr('agent posts', q("SELECT * FROM agent_social_media_posts ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 10"), 10000)
pr('recent social posts 78-84', q("SELECT * FROM social_media_posts WHERE day BETWEEN 78 AND 84 ORDER BY day DESC, post_id DESC LIMIT 80"), 30000)
pr('config overrides recent', q("SELECT * FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 30"), 20000)
pr('research projects', q("SELECT * FROM research_projects ORDER BY started_day DESC,tier"), 15000)
try: pr('cost_info', nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info(), 20000)
except Exception as e: print('cost info err', e)
try: pr('market_overview', nm.market.get_market_overview(), 30000)
except Exception as e: print('market overview err', e)
try: pr('research list', nm.research.list_research_projects(), 30000)
except Exception as e: print('research list err', e)
try: pr('social API', nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=100), 30000)
except Exception as e: print('social err', e)
